SPARQL query over wikidata:
```sparql
PREFIX wdt: <http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/>
PREFIX wd: <http://www.wikidata.org/entity/>
SELECT ?country WHERE {
  ?country wdt:P31 wd:Q112099
}
```

Using entities:
- island country (wd:Q112099), also known as "borderless country", "borderless nation", "borderless state", "island nation", "island state":
  - is instance of administrative territorial entity type
  - is subclass of country
  - is subclass of state
  - state whose primary territory consists of one or more islands or parts of islands

Using properties:
- instance of (wd:P31 as wdt), also known as "example of", "has type", "is a", "is a[n]", "is an", etc.:
  - type to which this subject corresponds/belongs. Different ... is an instance of mountain; volcano is a subclass of mountain

messages.content:
You are a question answering assistant. Your job is to generate a SPARQL query to answer a given user question.

You should follow a step-by-step approach to generate the SPARQL query:
1. Determine possible entities and properties implied by the user question.
2. Search for the entities and properties in the knowledge graphs. Where applicable, constrain the searches with already identified entities and properties.
3. Gradually build up the SPARQL query using the identified entities and properties. Start with simple queries and add more complexity as needed. Execute intermediate queries to get feedback and to verify your assumptions. You may need to refine or rethink your current plan based on the query results and go back to step 2 if needed, possibly multiple times.
4. Use the answer or cancel function to finalize your answer and stop the generation process.

Available knowledge graphs:
- "wikidata" at https://qlever.dev/api/wikidata
  - "entity" index (type="fuzzy"): Entities indexed by their labels and synonyms
  - "property" index (type="embedding", modalities="text"): Properties indexed by their labels, synonyms, and identifiers

Index types used:
- "embedding": Retrieves items by cosine similarity between their embeddings and the query embedding. The embedding model used depends on the index and may support text, images, or both.
- "fuzzy": Retrieves items by overlap between their label words and the query keywords. The query keywords must not match label words exactly, but some fuzziness is allowed. The longer a query keyword is, the more it can deviate from a label word and still be considered a match, though it will also contribute less to the overall score. No special query operators like AND/OR are supported.

SPARQL prefixes for use in function calls:
- bd: http://www.bigdata.com/rdf#
- cc: http://creativecommons.org/ns#
- data: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityData/
- dc: http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/
- dct: http://purl.org/dc/terms/
- foaf: http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/
- geo: http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#
- geof: http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/
- gn: http://www.geonames.org/ontology#
- hint: http://www.bigdata.com/queryHints#
- imdb: https://www.imdb.com/
- math: http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions/math#
- ontolex: http://www.w3.org/ns/lemon/ontolex#
- owl: http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#
- p: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/
- pq: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/
- pqn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value-normalized/
- pqv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value/
- pr: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/
- prn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value-normalized/
- prov: http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#
- prv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value/
- ps: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/
- psn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value-normalized/
- psv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value/
- qb: http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#
- qfn: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/function#
- ql: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/builtin-functions/
- qlss: https://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/spatialSearch/
- rdf: http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#
- rdfs: http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
- ref: http://www.wikidata.org/reference/
- s: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/statement/
- schema: http://schema.org/
- skos: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#
- v: http://www.wikidata.org/value/
- void: http://rdfs.org/ns/void#
- wd: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/
- wdno: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/novalue/
- wdt: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/
- wdtn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct-normalized/
- wikibase: http://wikiba.se/ontology#
- xsd: http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#

Additional rules to follow:
- Explain your thought process before and after each step and function call.
- Do not ask the user for clarification until you have made an initial attempt at completing the task. When the task input is incomplete or ambiguous, proceed based on reasonable assumptions.
- Do not use "SERVICE wikibase:label { bd:serviceParam wikibase:language ..." in SPARQL queries. It is unsupported by the used QLever SPARQL endpoints. Use rdfs:label or similar properties to get labels instead.
- Always execute your final SPARQL query before giving an answer to make sure it returns the expected results.
- The SPARQL query should always return the actual identifiers / IRIs of the items in its result. It additionally may return labels or other human-readable information, but they are optional and should be put within optional clauses unless explicitly requested by the user.
- Do not stop early if there are still obvious improvements to be made to the SPARQL query. For example, keep refining your SPARQL query if its result contains irrelevant items or is missing items you expected.
- Do not perform additional computation (e.g. filtering, sorting, calculations) on the result of the SPARQL query to determine the answer. All computation should be done solely within SPARQL.
- For questions with a "True" or "False" answer the SPARQL query should be an ASK query.

reasoning.content:
Okay, let's tackle this question: "What countries are island countries?" Hmm. So, I need to find all countries that are island countries. First, I should figure out what "island country" means in the context of Wikidata.

Island countries are countries that are entirely made up of islands, right? Not just countries with islands, but countries where the entire territory is islands. Examples would be Japan, Madagascar, Indonesia, maybe the Philippines. But wait, some countries have islands but are also on a continent, like the US (Hawaii) or France (Réunion), but those aren't considered island countries because they have continental territories. So, the key is countries that are surrounded by water on all sides, with no land connection to a larger landmass.

In Wikidata, there's a property that might indicate this. Let me think. I remember that there's a "country" subclass under "sovereign state," but for island countries, maybe there's a specific property or a label in the country's entry. Wait, maybe the property "island country" exists. Let me check using the tools available.

First, I should search for the appropriate property in Wikidata. The user mentioned using search_property to find properties related to "island country." Let me try that.

Wait, the functions include search_property. Let me call search_property with query "island country" to see if there's a specific property.

So, I'll execute search_property(kg="wikidata", query="island country"). Let me imagine the result. Maybe it returns a property like "P1701" which is "island country" in Wikidata. Wait, but I should check.

Alternatively, maybe "island country" is a category or a label. But properties usually have QIDs. Let's proceed with the function call.

Wait, the assistant is supposed to make actual tool calls. So, the correct step is to use search_property first to find the right property.

So, first step: search_property("wikidata", "island country").

Assuming the response has the property, say, wdt:P1701, then the next step is to find all countries (P31 = Q6256, which is country) that have this property.

Wait, countries in Wikidata are instances of country (P31 wd:Q6256). So, the SPARQL would select ?country where ?country wdt:P31 wd:Q6256 and ?country wdt:P1701 ?island.

Wait, but P1701 is the property for "island country" (I think that's the actual QID). But I need to confirm.

Alternatively, maybe the property is called "island country" with a different ID. Let's do the search.

tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. applies to jurisdiction (wd:P1001 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "Of country"), also known as "applicable area", "applicable country", "applicable geographic place", "applicable jurisdiction", "applicable location", etc.:
  - the item (institution, law, public office, public register, ... jurisdiction: a country, state, municipality, etc)
2. basin country (wd:P205 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "basin countries":
  - country that have drainage to/from or border the body of water
3. located in/on physical feature (wd:P706 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "located on island"), also known as "geographical region", "is in", "is on", "landform", "located on island", etc.:
  - located on the specified (geo)physical feature. Should not be ... range (P4552). Use P206 for things in/on bodies of water.
4. country of citizenship (wd:P27 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "subject of (country)"), also known as "citizen of", "citizenship", "country", "(legal) nationality", "nation", etc.:
  - the object is a country that recognizes the subject as its citizen
5. contains the administrative territorial entity (wd:P150 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "has countries"), also known as "contains", "contains administrative territorial entity", "divided into", "divides into", "has administrative divisions", etc.:
  - (list of) direct subdivisions of an administrative territorial entity
6. country of origin (wd:P495 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "homeland"), also known as "comes from", "CoO", "country", "homeland", "originates from", etc.:
  - country of origin of this item (creative work, food, phrase, product, etc.)
7. continent (wd:P30 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv):
  - continent of which the subject is a part
8. country (wd:P17 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "host country", "land", "sovereign state", "state":
  - sovereign state that this item is in (not to be used for human beings)
9. valid in place (wd:P3005 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "country"), also known as "applies to country", "applies to location", "applies to nonjurisdictional location", "applies to nonjurisdictional place", "applies to place", etc.:
  - place where a statement is valid
10. operating area (wd:P2541 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "country served"), also known as "area of operations", "area of responsibility", "area operated", "area served", "areas served", etc.:
  - geographic area or jurisdiction an organisation or industry operates in, serves, or has responsibility for

question: What countries are island countries ?
reference_answer: The northern part of the island of Cyprus is the de facto independent state of Northern Cyprus, which is recognized only by Turkey. In the south of the island are the Sovereign Base Areas of Akrotiri and Dhekelia, controlled by the United Kingdom. | In 1983, Northern Cyprus declared independence from Cyprus. Northern Cyprus sovereignty has been recognized by only one United Nations member state (Turkey). It is not a member state of the United Nations. Most states recognize Cyprus claim to sovereignty over Northern Cyprus. | Since the conclusion of the Chinese Civil War, the Republic of China (ROC) retains actual rule over the islands of Taiwan, Penghu, Kinmen, the Matsu, and other minor islands, all of which are collectively known as "Free Area" or Taiwan Area in contrast to Mainland China being under communist rule, thus making it a de facto island nation, and has become known colloquially as simply "Taiwan" due to the island of Taiwan forming the majority of the ROC-controlled territories; nonetheless, the ROC has not officially renounced its constitutional-defined territories which include areas effectively controlled by the [[China|Peoples Republic of China]], Mongolia, Tuva (a Russian republic) etc. If claimed territories were taken into account, the ROC would not be a borderless country, nor a country centred around a major island. The ROC-controlled territories are also claimed by the Peoples Republic of China. See: Political status of Taiwan, Two Chinas, One-China policy, and Cross-strait relations. | Martinique | Administered by Norway under the Svalbard Treaty.
